title: Billie Eilish Slams ICE During Song of the Year Grammys Speech
text:
Billie Eilish's Grammy Statement on ICE

Song of the Year Win: Billie Eilish won Song of the Year at the 2026 Grammy Awards for "Wildflower," her fourth single from the 2024 album Hit Me Hard and Soft. This marks her third Song of the Year victory.

ICE Commentary: During her acceptance speech, Eilish made a strong political statement about immigration enforcement. She declared: "No one is illegal on stolen land" and called for continued activism, saying "our voices really do matter." Both she and her brother Finneas wore "ICE Out" pins while accepting the award.

Broader Context: Multiple artists at the 2026 Grammys spoke against Trump administration immigration policies. Bad Bunny and Olivia Dean also referenced immigration issues during their acceptance speeches, with Dean highlighting her immigrant heritage.
